senses.definition: a device that can send and receive documents in electronic form using a telephone line and then print them

examples.x: He sent the contract via fax to ensure it reached the client promptly.
The fax machine beeped as it received the document from the sender.
The office has a dedicated fax line for sending and receiving important documents.
The fax machine at the doctor's office hummed quietly as it transmitted medical records.

senses.definition: a phone that we can carry with us and use anywhere because it has no wires

examples.x: Can you please mute your cell phone during the movie?
He dropped his cell phone and cracked the screen.
I forgot to bring my cell phone to the meeting.
My cell phone battery is running low.
She rarely uses her cell phone for making calls, mostly for texting.

senses.definition: a communication device used for talking to people who are far away and also have a similar device

examples.x: He needs to recharge his telephone battery.
She picked up the telephone to call her friend.
She received a call from an unknown number on her telephone.
The telephone rang, but no one answered.
They recorded the conversation on the telephone for future reference.

senses.definition: a type of telephone that uses Internet protocol to transmit voice calls over the Internet, rather than traditional telephone lines
a wireless communication device allowing two or more people to communicate over a shared frequency

examples.x: The security team relied on their two-way radios to coordinate their movements around the building.
The workers used a two-way radio to communicate with each other across the construction site.
During the hike, we carried two-way radios to stay in touch in case we got separated.
At the theme park, staff members communicated using two-way radios to keep everything running smoothly.

senses.definition: a portable two-way radio used for communication between two or more people within a certain range

examples.x: We used walkie-talkies to stay in touch while hiking through the mountains.
The construction crew used walkie-talkies to communicate across the noisy site.
The security team at the mall relies on walkie-talkies to coordinate their efforts.
The walkie-talkies allowed the event organizers to stay in touch with volunteers all day long.

senses.definition: a communication system that allows people in different parts of a plane, office, etc. to speak to each other

examples.x: The office installed an intercom system so employees could communicate with each other easily across different rooms.
I used the intercom system to let the delivery driver know where to leave the package.
He pressed the button on the intercom system to ask if anyone was available for a meeting.
The intercom system in the hospital makes it easy for staff to communicate in different departments.
She was able to speak to her neighbor through the intercom system without leaving her apartment.

senses.definition: a technology that enables real-time communication and collaboration between two or more people using video, audio, and chat features, regardless of their location

examples.x: We used a video conferencing system to attend the team meeting from home today.
The video conferencing system works really well for our remote work meetings.
Due to the weather, we had to switch our in-person meeting to a video conferencing system.
I had trouble logging in to the video conferencing system for the online class this morning.
She demonstrated how to use the video conferencing system during the training session.

senses.definition: a machine that answers missed calls and records the messages callers leave

examples.x: She checked her answering machine for messages after coming home.
He set up his answering machine to greet callers with a friendly message.
They listened to the messages on the answering machine from the weekend.
She was pleased to hear a message from an old friend on the answering machine.
They relied on the answering machine to capture important calls.

senses.definition: a small electronic device that generates beeping sounds to signal one should call someone

examples.x: When I worked in the hospital, the nurses used their beepers to get emergency updates.
My grandfather still has his old beeper from when he worked as a paramedic.
In the 90s, we all carried beepers to stay in touch when we were out of the office.
The emergency room staff relies on beepers to quickly respond to urgent situations.
He was so used to his beeper that he felt lost without it when it broke.

senses.definition: a mobile phone that uses satellites for communication in remote areas where traditional cellular networks are not available

examples.x: She bought a satellite phone for her trip to the Arctic, as regular cell phones wouldn't work there.
Satellite phones are essential for expeditions in areas where there is no mobile network.
If you travel to remote islands, a satellite phone might be your only way to stay connected.
He carried a satellite phone while hiking across the desert to stay in touch with the base camp.
The rescue team communicated with headquarters using satellite phones during the storm.

senses.definition: a telephone in a public place that one needs to pay for, mostly by prepaid cards

examples.x: He searched for a payphone to call for help after his car broke down on the highway.
The hotel lobby still had a functional payphone for guests who didn't have cell phones.
She dug through her purse for change to use the payphone to inform her parents of her late arrival.
The payphone booth on the corner was a relic of the past, rarely used in the age of smartphones.
He used the payphone outside the convenience store to call his friend and arrange a meeting spot.

senses.definition: an international system of sending and receiving messages using a phone line and a teletypewriter
a communication device that uses telegraph technology to send and receive text messages over long distances, once used before the advent of fax machines and email

examples.x: The telex machine in the office was used to send urgent messages to overseas clients.
He learned to operate a telex machine during his training in international trade.
She typed the contract details on the telex machine and sent them to the company’s branch office.
The museum displayed a telex machine to showcase early methods of global communication.

senses.definition: a portable device that combines the functions of a cell phone and a computer, such as browsing the Internet, using apps, making calls, etc.

examples.x: She relied on her smartphone to manage her schedule and stay connected.
His smartphone had countless apps, from social media to productivity tools.
They used their smartphones to navigate through the unfamiliar city.
The smartphone's camera quality rivaled that of a professional camera.
He couldn't imagine a day without using his smartphone for work and leisure.

senses.definition: a small computer that you can take with you wherever you go, and it sits on your lap or a table so you can use it

examples.x: He bought a new laptop with better processing speed.
He opened his laptop to check his emails and messages.
I need to charge my laptop; the battery is running low.
My brother uses his laptop for studying and online classes.
She carries her laptop with her wherever she goes.

senses.definition: an electronic device that stores and processes data

examples.x: He upgraded the computer's software for better performance.
I use my computer to browse the internet and check emails.
She customized the desktop wallpaper on her computer.
She uses a computer to create digital art.
The computer has a large storage capacity for files.

senses.definition: a flat, small, portable computer that one controls and uses by touching its screen

examples.x: She uses her tablet to take notes during lectures, finding it more convenient than a laptop.
The new tablet features a high-resolution display, making it perfect for watching movies and playing games.
He prefers reading e-books on his tablet because it's easier to carry around than physical books.
Many artists use a tablet with a stylus to create digital artwork and illustrations.
The tablet's battery lasts for up to ten hours, allowing users to work or browse without needing to recharge frequently.

senses.definition: a method of communication that uses radio signals, instead of wires, to send out data

examples.x: The wireless allowed soldiers to communicate over long distances during the war.
The ship's crew relied on wireless to send distress signals during the storm.
The invention of wireless made it possible to broadcast radio programs to a wide audience.

senses.definition: a form of visual communication that involves sending a message through smoke from a fire

examples.x: The tribe used a smoke signal to warn neighboring villages of approaching danger.
The hikers lit a fire to create a smoke signal when they got lost in the forest.
Long ago, tribes communicated across valleys by sending a smoke signal.
The rescue team spotted a smoke signal from the stranded campers on the mountain.
When stranded in the wilderness, they built a fire to create a smoke signal for rescuers.

senses.definition: a rock dove with great homing abilities that is trained to carry messages
a person who carries a message or information from one person or place to another

examples.x: The messenger delivered the package to my office this morning.
She was hired as a messenger to carry documents between the two departments.
The messenger ran quickly to ensure the letter was delivered before the deadline.
I used to work as a messenger, delivering messages between businesses in the city.

senses.definition: a person who makes public announcements or proclamations in a loud voice in a public space before modern forms of mass communication

examples.x: The town crier stood in the square, announcing the arrival of the royal visitors.
Everyone gathered around the town crier to hear the latest proclamation from the mayor.
As the town crier read out the news, people stopped their activities to listen closely.
The sound of the town crier's bell echoed through the streets, signaling an important message.

senses.definition: a type of percussion instrument that is used in various cultures in Africa and other parts of the world to send messages over long distances by imitating the tone and rhythm of spoken language

examples.x: The talking drum echoed through the village, carrying the chief's announcement.
In ancient times, a talking drum was used to send messages over long distances.
He used the talking drum to communicate with neighboring tribes during his travels.
She learned to play the talking drum as part of her cultural heritage.

senses.definition: a system of signaling using flags or other devices to communicate messages over a long distance

examples.x: Before radios, they relied on semaphore to send urgent messages across the sea.

senses.definition: a communication system by which people were able to send and receive messages over long distances, using electrical or radio signals

examples.x: The old train station still has a room dedicated to the telegraph, showcasing relics from the past.
He visited the museum to learn about the history of the telegraph and its impact on communication.

senses.definition: a system of communication that uses dots and dashes to represent letters, numbers, and symbols, enabling messages to be transmitted over long distances through sound or light signals

examples.x: He found an old book about Morse code and was fascinated by how people used it to communicate.
The pilot used Morse code to send a message when the radio stopped working mid-flight.
They practiced using Morse code during their scout meetings to improve their communication skills.
When the phone lines were down, they used a flashlight to signal in Morse code.
She learned Morse code as part of her training to become a radio operator.

senses.definition: a form of communication that uses whistling to imitate spoken language and transmit messages over long distances in hilly or mountainous areas

examples.x: In the mountains, villagers use whistled language to communicate across valleys.
The villagers rely on whistled language when they need to communicate without being heard by others.
Whistled language is not just for practical purposes, but also plays an important role in cultural traditions.
A skilled whistler can use a whistled language to convey complex messages, much like spoken words.

senses.definition: a telephone with a microphone and a loudspeaker that can be used without the need to be hold near one's ear

examples.x: We used the speakerphone during the meeting so everyone in the room could hear the conversation.
Can you put the phone on speakerphone so we can all hear the instructions?
I left my phone on speakerphone so I could cook dinner while talking to my friend.
He turned on the speakerphone so that his whole team could join the call without passing the phone around.
I prefer using the speakerphone in the car so I can talk hands-free while driving.

senses.definition: a written or printed message that is sent to someone or an organization, company, etc.

examples.x: I received a thank-you letter from the charity I donated to.
He sent a letter of resignation to his boss.
He was delighted to get a letter of acceptance from the university.
I received a letter from my friend who lives abroad.

senses.definition: a system that is used to send and receive messages or documents via a network

examples.x: He signed up for the newsletter and receives updates via email.
I use email to share photos with my family and friends.
She received an important document via email.
We exchanged contact information through email.
We use email to communicate with our colleagues at work.

senses.definition: the transmission of information, data, or messages over a distance through the use of electronic or optical signals, media, and technologies
the act of exchanging text, multimedia content, or information between individuals or groups through digital communication platforms, such as messaging apps, social media, or email
